content.description:
Idea: generate SVG diagrams from item data, rendered via functional transclusion or custom views. No new storage needed — diagrams are computed live from existing item metadata.

## Motivation

Hobson's reactive wiring (events, watches, dependencies) is spread across many items and two mechanisms (declarative watches + programmatic `events.on()`). No single place shows the full picture. Auto-generated diagrams would make system structure visible at a glance.

## Diagram Types (by priority)

### 1. Event Flow Diagram

Visualize the full emit-subscribe wiring: which items emit which events, which items subscribe (with filter conditions), and how events flow through the system.

**Data sources:**
- Declarative: `content.watches` arrays (machine-readable, ~15 items)
- Programmatic: `events.on()` and `events.emit()` calls in `content.code` (~6 items)
- Event type hierarchy: `e0e00000-*` items define the event tree

**Why useful:** The reactive system is invisible without reading every item. Filter conditions (`type`, `typeExtends`, `id`) on watches add specificity that's easy to miss. The full wiring spans ~20 items and two mechanisms.

### 2. Code Dependency Graph

Visualize `api.require()` relationships between code items. ~63 items have dependencies.

**Natural clusters:** editor cluster (field-view-* -> codemirror), Hob cluster (hob-*-tests -> hob-interpreter), view boilerplate (*-view-* -> generic-view), system cluster (keyboard-shortcuts -> context-menu-lib -> item-search-lib).

**Hub libraries:** generic-view (10 dependents), test-lib (14), hob-interpreter (12), item-search-lib (7), modal-lib (7).

**Why useful:** "What breaks if I change this library?" Reverse dependency lookup. Cluster identification. The data is non-obvious without reading every code item.

### 3. Item Relationship Graph (proof of concept)

Generic visualization of attachments, tags, type chains, and `item://` references for a given item. Pretty but often not useful in practice — the spatial canvas and tag browser already show most of this.

## Implementation Approach

### Phase 1: Declarative event flow (proof of concept) (IMPLEMENTED)

A `diagram-lib` library item that generates inline SVG. Consume via functional transclusion: `![](item://diagram-lib?call=eventFlow)`. Reads only declarative `content.watches` — no code analysis needed.

![](item://d1a00000-0001-0000-0000-000000000000?call=eventFlow)

### Phase 2: Extend symbol extractor for `_deps`

Add a `content._deps` field alongside `content._symbols`, auto-generated by the existing [symbol-indexer-watch](item://ac0ac0ac-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) infrastructure:

```json
"_deps": {
  "requires": ["generic-view", "modal-lib"],
  "subscribes": [{ "event": "ITEM_UPDATED", "raw": "EVENT_IDS.ITEM_UPDATED" }],
  "emits": [{ "event": "SELECTION_CHANGED" }]
}
```

The Acorn AST already has `CallExpression` nodes — add cases for `*.require(StringLiteral)`, `*.events.on(...)`, `*.events.emit(...)`. The [symbol-extractor-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) currently only captures definition sites, not call sites, but the walker infrastructure handles it.

Challenge: resolving `EVENT_IDS.ITEM_UPDATED` constants to actual event GUIDs requires knowledge of the EVENT_IDS map from [kernel:core](item://33333333-1111-0000-0000-000000000000).

### Phase 3: Full diagrams

With `_deps` on every code item, both the event flow and dependency graph become trivial queries over item metadata. Add layout algorithms, interactivity (click to navigate), and cluster detection.

## Design Considerations

- **SVG over Canvas** — SVG is text, renders natively in DOM, works with transclusion, no binary storage needed
- **Live vs static** — Functional transclusion re-executes on render, so diagrams auto-update. But scanning all items is O(n). Consider caching or limiting scope.
- **Interactivity** — SVG elements can have click handlers for navigation (`api.navigate()`). Hovering could show item details.
- **Layout** — Force-directed for relationship graphs, layered/hierarchical for event flow and dependency DAGs. Could use a simple custom layout or a library like dagre (if added as an item).

## See Also

- [Event System](item://c0c0c0c0-0006-0000-0000-000000000000) — Event types and declarative watches
- [symbol-extractor-lib](item://ac0ac0ac-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — Existing Acorn-based code analysis
- [symbol-indexer-watch](item://ac0ac0ac-0004-0000-0000-000000000000) — Auto-indexing on code item changes
- [Transclusion](item://c0c0c0c0-0035-0000-0000-000000000000) — Functional transclusion for rendering diagrams
- [Hob Design Direction](item://c0c0c0c0-0058-0000-0000-000000000000) — Related: Hob as the language for user-facing tools
